dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3103 IS: To authorize the extension of nondiscriminatory treatment (normal trade relations treatment) to products of certain countries.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Daines (for himself, Mr. Risch, and Mr. Murphy) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To authorize the extension of nondiscriminatory treatment (normal trade relations treatment) to products of certain countries.

section.header: Termination of application of title IV of the Trade Act of 1974 to products of certain countries

subsection.header: Presidential determinations and extension of nondiscriminatory treatment
subsection.text: Notwithstanding any provision of title IV of the Trade Act of 1974 (19 U.S.C. 2431 et seq.), the President may—

paragraph.text: determine that such title should no longer apply to a covered country; and

after making a determination under paragraph (1) with respect to a covered country, proclaim the extension of nondiscriminatory treatment (normal trade relations treatment) to the products of the covered country.

subsection.header: Termination of applicability of title IV
subsection.text: On and after the effective date under subsection (a)(2) of the extension of nondiscriminatory treatment to the products of a covered country, title IV of the Trade Act of 1974 (19 U.S.C. 2431 et seq.) shall cease to apply to the covered country.
In this section, the term covered country means any country excluding Belarus, Cuba, and North Korea.
